title: City of Vernon IT Internship
description:
During my time as an IT intern for the City of Vernon I worked with a small team of 5 (including myself). Because of the small team dynamic, my team taught me more than just helpdesk.

 By the end of my internship I was able to troubleshoot a wide range of hardware or software issues for over 300 employees and on average, resolve 25%-30% of my team's tickets per month.

 I worked with established IT procedures to set up, deploy, and if needed troubleshoot, devices such as Windows 11/10/8/7 Desktop PC’s, laptops, tablets, monitors,printers, iOS devices, NVR/DVR's, CCTV's. I became in charge of imaging Desktop's/Laptop's using Windows Deployment Services, setting up and deploying mobile devices using AirWatch, I assisted on major projects where we needed to install multiple workstations which included (and not limited to) Desktops, Laptops, Mitel phones, printers. Often these workstations required network drops, running new cat cables, managed/unmanaged switches, POE injectors, etc.

 Prepared detailed work reports of all IT incidents that have been worked on, listing actions performed to supplies used to resolve the issue, all via a service desk ticketing system. On average closing out 40 tasks per week.

 I assisted on writing python scripts to better increase both myself and my team's workflow. Scripts that I assisted on are: 

 Tel_Script: This Python script is a GUI application for updating a telephone directory stored in a Microsoft Access database. Users can input or modify information, including full names, extensions, direct lines, and locations. After entering the data, they can submit the changes. The script provides a confirmation popup upon successful updates. It employs the tkinter library for the graphical user interface and pyodbc for database connectivity. The application streamlines the process of maintaining and updating telephone directory entries with a user-friendly interface. 

 KillTask: The Kill Task Script is a PowerShell tool that enables users to manage and troubleshoot tasks and processes on remote devices. It provides a user-friendly interface for connecting to a remote device, listing running processes, and terminating specific tasks. Users can choose from a menu of options, including terminating common applications prone to freezing, listing all running processes, or manually entering process names to kill. The script runs in a loop, allowing users to perform multiple actions on the same or different devices. It's a practical tool for remote task management and troubleshooting.

title: Army Research Lab
description: The purpose of SUDO is to be able to employ an algorithm that can calculate distances in which  a SUAS could be perceived by human targets on the drone, given parameters of auditory and visual footprints of a SUAS, as well as background and environmental auditory and visual data given to us by the Army Research Lab. This data will then be relayed to the 3D environment portion of our software, which can render a 3D perspective of detection zones and probabilities of detection within those zones.

title: Small Projects
description:
Temperature Converter: This is a web application that allows users to convert temperatures between Celsius, Fahrenheit, and Kelvin.

 Weather App: The Weather App is a web application that provides real-time weather information for locations around the world. It allows users to check the current weather conditions, temperature, and more. The app is built using React.js and utilizes the OpenWeatherMap API to fetch weather data.
